text: Overcharged by $480 for equipment I didnt have. Their response, not our problem. Awful, awful corporate customer service.

T-Mobile is crap, besides not working in many places, they simply cut my line because my card didn't work, but they didn't send me a message, letter or call to let me know that there was a payment error, they simply deactivated all the accounts. Disappointed, I've been a customer of this company for many years and today was the last straw, I'm going to look for another company that respects its customers like they say they do, because after this one I saw how much they appreciate their customers. I don't recommend it, and I never will. Simply disappointed. And look, only one payment wasn't released because of a problem with the card, but they didn't even send a message beforehand to let me know that the line could be suspended. They simply suspended it, and I was left without contact. Terrible!

I sent the signal device to T-Mobile and they received it on January 3rd. This is an old machine that was given to me 10 years ago. T-Mobile took $414 off my credit card without permission. Until now, they have not returned my money, making the excuse that the refund was not possible because they did not scan the machine. Every time I call, the staff just lies. 
How can I get this money back?
I'm really angry and disappointed.

I needed  a simple thing like a new screen savior for my Z flip 5.Went to store useless. Sent me to different store ,useless. I had to call T mobile. They  are going to send a new one for $53. Unbelievable for a $1500 phone. Been with T mobile for 17yrs. I'm leaving.  THEY SUCK!!!!

I’ve been a LOYAL customer for almost 7 years. What has kept me such a loyal customer was their excellent customer care. However, over the past year or two, I have experienced a great decline in customer satisfaction. Today, I was extremely dissatisfied more than twice. First the rep was rude and wouldn't let me speak after I made a payment under the impression to restore services. Although restored,  I attempted to contact a supervisor regarding my experience only for the rep to put her male colleague on the phone posing as a supervisor because obviously the call wasn't transferred …… At this point I went along with it bc I knew it was recorded …….On my 3rd attempt I finally spoke to a REAL supervisor but T MOBILE WILL NO LONGER GET MY BUSINESS because after I requested the REAL supervisor information she pretended to send her contact via text. I told her that I didn't receive it but instead she sent a no-reply email which had lots of misspelled words with little details and contact information. So long T MOBILE

Good aspect - you can get through to T-mobile customer services quickly.

Poor aspect - Wasn't receiving international calls so called T-mobile. Was told i needed to take out a international package costing an additional $15.00/month. I reluctantly agreed taking my monthly bill to $65.00/month.  I needed to confirm my ID with my non-US bank through automated system. this call automatically went through to answer phone.  on complaining to T-mobile i was told i needed to add extra money to my account to receive international call from international organizations and companies!    Total rip off.

In July 2023, I ordered an Apple Watch Ultra through T-Mobile. Unfortunately, in transit, the package was stolen by, I assume, one of the carriers employees. I called T-Mobile customer service and explained the situation, and they assured me that they input it in their system, and then I wouldn’t be charged for the watch because I never received it. This situation happened twice. The first time
they correctly, put a stop on the order. The second time (and this is the subject of the complaint.) the customer agent did not correctly put a stop on the charge. Because of this, T-Mobile has been charging me, without my consent and without my knowledge, $25 a month for the past 20 months. I realized this around September or October 2024. After six or seven phone calls with customer service over the last six months, they have acknowledged their mistake and that I do not actually have the product (and never actually even received the product) that they have been charging for. Notwithstanding their admission that they are charging me for a product that I never received, they have refused to refund me the approximately$500 they have taken from me, and they also refuse to take the future installment charges for said product off of my account. So to this day, they remain charging me for said product. I have proof that I have never received the product, and their customer service agents and investigation teams have even confirmed this through the use the electronic IMEI numbers on my account. Summarily, t-Mobile has acknowledged that I never received the product, and that they have been mistakenly charging me for said product for almost 2 years; however, they refuse to refund me as well as put a stop charge on future payments. This amounts to theft, plain and simple. I have spent the last 5 to 6 months trying to be reasonable with them, but to no avail.

I switched from Verizon to t-mobile because i was traveling out of the country most of the year. The offer was better so I thought. I’ve been extremely dissatisfied with T-Mobile's service. Not only has my bill been manipulated, but the overall service quality has been subpar. As a loyal customer, I expect transparency and reliable service, neither of which I have received. It's disappointing and has left me questioning my decision to stay with this company. I hope T-Mobile takes this feedback seriously to improve its customer experience. I am looking for another provider. I cannot recommend this company.

Joel Almanza with the T-Mobile store at 2510 Cherry Avenue in Long Beach, CA 90755 made my day a lot brighter with his efficient, fast and cordial customer service.  Hours earlier I had arrived in Long Beach from a two-year stay in Peru.  In addition to being in a different time zone and exhausted from a nine-hour flight, I had struggled unsuccessfully for 45 minutes trying to remove the SIM card from my iPhone 11, so I could switch SIM cards and change from my Perú service provider to my T-Mobile account for my life here in Long Beach.  It may seem like a small thing to those reading this review, but I think most would agree that a fully functioning cellular with access to all the services it provides is essential to a good day.  So, with my frustration at an indescribable level, I went to the T-Mobile store and presented my problem to Joel.  He assured me he would solve my problem in no time and was happy to do it.  And he did.  And, since you can’t see it, let me assure you that there is a smile on my face as I write this.  Thank you, Joel.

I have been trying for a couple months to get onto the home Wi-Fi app on my mobile device so I can control my home Wi-Fi but I cannot get on it I've reset my admin app and everything and it still will not let me on I have 12 devices connected to my home Wi-Fi and I can't see who any of them are I can't disconnect them I have no control over it my devices that I have have been hacked when they're connected to my home Wi-Fi I would never recommend T-Mobile to anyone they are horrible and when I say horrible I'm putting it very nicely I'm trying to be very professional right here but they suck and not in a good way please don't ever use them

We purchased two 30-day international phone plans at $50 per phone for our trip to the UK. It was imperative that we had these plans. However, on using our phones in the UK we were informed the numbers dialed were not recognized and informed to check the number dialed. I added the +44 and when no connection tried the number without the country code. Again, no connection and the same response from T-Mobile to check number. Called TM and was put through to the tech department. I was on the phone for at least 40 minutes as the tech was having difficulty in rectifying an error he found. He also noticed I had purchased 3 international plans! He also said we can sort out all the charges on our return to the US. Anyway, he stated it would take a while to figure out the issue and would call back. When he called back, I was unable to answer the call. He tried my wife's phone, same issue. I called TM again and explained the situation. "OK I will leave a message for him to call you back." Really! I said. "We can't answer when he calls" We went round and round with no solution. Being exasperated I stated that we needed to call the US due to private concerns and I needed to call relatives, hotels etc while in the UK. 
On our return and explaining to TM the poor service received and that I paid $100 for a service that was deficient I wanted a credit along with any roaming phone charges we had to make due to their fault that the international call plans failed. Their response was "You can have a $20 credit" To top it all I received a text on 16th January stating "Your Int  30 day Pass starts now" and another immediately stating the pass has ended. Odd as I enacted the 30 days as of 1st January 2025!!

I purchased an A15 at Walmart that was supposed to be free, got charged $50. I was told the plan was $21 for text/talk (no data), i was put on essentials for $60 a month with everything unlimited. I was told there was no contract. There is a 24 month contract. I called T-mobile to find out whats going on and waited on hold for 59 minutes before I got to a manager who after I told him what happened, the manager decided to just hang up on me. I will be returning the phone and immediately reporting T-Mobile here, BBB, and everywhere I can for lying to my face about the cost, plan, and contract.  I contacted my bank and informed them of the deception as well and put in a complaint for the charge. I have NEVER been repeated lied to my face about any service. I got the phone as a cheap emergency phone with the basics and basics and got maxed out by T-Mobile with their agent lying about the entire process. I went online and phone an A13 phone for $35 on offerup. the A15 is NOT free. The A15 WILL FORCE you into a 24-month contract. The entire process was fraudulent and criminal. Being hung up on by the manager while I was trying to get this resolved was so disrespectful and proves the process is a scam. Stay away from this company at all costs.

Unfortunately I closed my account on the first week of cycle, and they charged me for entire month. No proration. And to at insult to injury, they gave my account to a collection firm even though I paid. When I called the collection to resolve they said they can't find the reference number they sent me on their system. When I called tmobile to resolve they said my account is no longer in their system.

I’ve been with T-Mobile for a while and I’m finding that they have great promotions but their service actually sucks, their towers are always down, my app hasn’t worked since they switched to t-life back in November they never test anything before forcing it on their customers and you have to constantly have them check their billing, I was supposed to be on a $30.00 per month internet contract they changed they box and increased my fee. The only time they send you a survey is when they’ve done something right. When they can’t help you don’t hear from them for days and the only thing they say we will issue a credit. It’s Sports season I don’t want credit I want to watch games and stream movies with uninterrupted service when the issue are pending they lose the previous tickets and only deal with the most current even if they are intertwined I’m dumping them.

I have been a customer for 10 years. Over the last 6 months, every few weeks, my phone could only connect to Google services and nothing else. I couldn’t even open Spotify. I had to call customer service, and they wasted 30 minutes of my time before finally deciding to reset their side of the network. This temporarily fixed the problem for a few weeks, but it kept recurring. Every time, they submitted and escalated tickets to their engineering team, yet the issue remains unresolved after 6 months. I am now switching to another provider.

They didn’t refund my $10 balance, and their excuse is that I had closed my account!

The worst
I have a $80 dollars plan last 3 months they been charging me over $200 you call them you get a call center from India that promises you the bill is gonna change the following month them you get charged the same bunch of rip off company 
The worst.

At my request, T-Mobile processed a suspension of my T-mobile home internet service and promised the monthly fee would be reduced from $50 to $10. As agreed, the service got suspended immediately but T-mobile continued to charge the full subscription amount of $50. When I called in again, the agent just said, sorry there was an update to our terms (= fineprint) and now when we suspend we continue to charge the full amount. Of course, this makes no sense whatsoever. Why even offer a suspension when you get charged no matter what. And not living up to the agreement is just fraudulent. Took me a lot of headaches and escalation to get the charge refunded and I just canceled my line. Goodbye T-Mobile. Never again! Current management seems to be on a mission to destroy all customer trust they once had.

Tried talking to customer service and am able to get anyone. Was talking to a Jay and he transferred me and no one ever came back on.  Very poor customer service I recommend do not sign up for the service as they do not stand behind anything once you sign up.  Impossible to speak with a human unless you go to a store hopefully you have 1 closer than 50 miles away.

edits.text: No customer service online only a virtual robot

text: They are a ridiculous and lying company! I made a cell phone plan and paid $127 every month for the plan, when the iPhone 16 was released they said that I could exchange the old device for the new one and I paid off the old device and got the new one and I would pay $127 dollars so I did I sent the device to them in the box that they sent me! I received 2 messages on the app saying that I had not yet sent the device and that my promotion would be canceled I called them and they informed me that it was already said that the device had already been received and that I did not need to worry 4 months would pass, I woke up my bill was $165 dollars. I went to the store to find out why, and the girl at the store told me it was because I hadn't sent the old device, so I had to cancel my contract. I had to pay $1,099 up front to keep my device, and I also lost my old device, which I had paid off because I sent it to them. People, don't sign a plan with T-Mobile, they don't honor the contract and in the end you'll lose. Look for another company. I looked and I'm satisfied with Verizon's service.

Lies lies lies lies. Anything that comes out of their mouth is a lie and hidden fee you won't know about. Was due for an upgrade got a new phone and was told that it was paid off and time for upgrade so I did months later being hit with double charges for my monthly bill and come to find out they're charging me for both phones still that's crazy. Bill was 280 for no reason other then being misinformed

Representative MARTASHA from T-mobile lie to us so we could switch companies, she bribe us by telling us we will get new iPhones if we trade our phones, but that was a lie. We were considering switching to T-mobile, but every single representative we spoke to had lie to us and everyone said different information. We returned all the equipment they sent to us, and now they want to charge a restocking fee, even after having proof on writing what the representative offered was a lie just to get us to signed. They were so many hidden fees that doesn’t make sense, she said we will pay a certain amount a month, and ended up being wayyy more money that she told us. I should have listened to the reviews, this company has to be shut down!! What a nightmare they are.

Do not use these people! They of all cellular companies are the worst by far! The give you a billing date that means nothing to them if you have autopay. They claim autopay gives them the right to debit the payment 3-5 days before the due date . Why have a due date? Why not 3-5 days after the due date? They are desperate people . They will even charge you 10.00 to take the payment over the phone a day early! Their contract and word is worth nothing , just a company run by losers.

You can tell the people they hired are paid by the hour. The last several interactions I've had are outsourced so you can not even understand half of what is being said (and I'm in customer service and normally can) then simple what should be 5 minutes calls are put on hold for things the "expert" should know like 14 minutes to see how many gigs of data I used last month. Another 17 minutes on hold to tell me that I can upgrade my plan for one month for my vacation to get extra gigs. SERIOUSLY? 49 minutes for this convo- I'm traveling to mexico next week- wanted to make sure my plan covers international travel.  Her you only have 5gigs of data- me- can I buy more data?  That was the extent in entirety for 49 minutes! Not including the hour wait to talk to a tech. The people answering the phones there are just milking the clock. Or just not familiar in the slightest with where they work.
F T-Mobile they are a rip off

They charged me for a year, 2 phone lines while I didn't have a single one! When I looked at the account, there's a random number I've never had! You'd think they'd apologize and refund me, but instead, they told me I should've checked my account! Pathetic! T-Mobile = Scammers

Absolutely the worst customer service. I had a problem with my billing so I called the customer service and the woman said yes she can see there's a problem but you can't do anything with it right now. She tells me she will call me back today at 5:00 p.m. so I wait all day at home and she never calls. All they do is lie to you. They don't care if you have to pay more and all I did because I had the Netflix with ads and for $8.50 it said I could have it without the commercials so I go ahead and do it. Big mistake charges me almost $15 instead of the $8.50. So what do I do I just have to go to another phone company and get rid of T-Mobile because they don't care about that they outsource their customer service overseas where they just don't care there. They try to act nice but all you hear is noise in the background because they're all laughing having a good time. They promise you the world but when you have to make it change your account you have to go to an account specialist so you get passed on another 10-15 minutes and then they lie to you.

Very very bad company! They sent me a device and when I returned it in same condition they charged me money for it saying that I damaged it! Despite the fact that it already arrived non working and damaged. And they use the fact that I cannot prove it because I took no pictures of it! Also they changed my plan without my knowledge and increased my monthly payment. Also one day I lost my internet driving in the middle of nowhere and lost my map. When I called them, they said that driver was disconnected?!!! What? So it was working before and someone disconnected it on purpose. When I asked for explanation, they couldn’t give one. But the last drop happened today. When I called the company and asked them to unlock my phone because I’m going to relatives funeral in Kiev, Ukraine, they said they would not do it because I’m on contract and it’s not up yet!! And on top of it they tried to sell me more international plans?!!! OMG. These people make money off of your pain!! STAY AWAY!

I had Home Gateway that quit <9 mos old. Took it to T-Mobile store. It took 2 hours. They tried to give me new one as free exchange, however it would not work. So they pulled second one from back but told me it would "cost me $11.04 because we already gave you one for free and we can't do it again" I never had 1st new one in my hands or left store! Then they said, "If you don't pay $11.04 then we can't give you Gateway. However, you can call T-mobile and order one for free that will come in 5 days." I called Customer Svc in front of them and they told store "you made a mistake and should not charge customer for second Gateway. There is a way to give it to him no-charge" Store refused and charged me. Customer svc said they could not refund charge because of way store charged me. I called Manager who came to store. He said he would " get refund to you in next 24 hours". He never did. I disputed charge on my credit card. Therefore, T-Mobile added to my monthly bill, charged $15 late fee, and did not give me my autopay discount for the month. Therefore, my money bill is $40 higher this month than normal. T-Mobile is just as BAD, if not worse than Verizon, ATT, etc!!!

Highly, highly highly recommend a HARD pass on Tmobile.  Their support is atrocious.  When it comes time to leave they make it brutal to reach them and do it and then they bill you for another couple of months and claim you didn't cancel.  Service is lousy. Go elsewhere - Dealt with Sam #4973207 and he truly had no ability to answer or resolve anything. Spent 2 hours the night before dealing with support person that was even less competent. There's a lot better and/or more affordable options.

This provider is a scam, cheat and a Lier.
 I have been with T-Mobile for several years with 3 lines, paying $105 a month which includes unlimited data.
Last year they changed my plan without telling me and said it was a special offer, I ended up paying more and had to call them to revert to my original plan.
 I  have been in the UK for a while now, they blocked my unlimited data and roaming service, they said I was using too much data! I called them they were rude and would not restore my data and roaming service. I would give them a BIG 0 STAR if I could. I will be looking for another provider when I go back. What a big SCAM company.

T-Mobile IS the very best mobile service we've EVER had. Always able to reach a live body when you call and no wasted time getting jacked around from one phone prompt to another. Their service works flawlessly when we bounce back and forth to Mexico and Canada. They're doing customer service right!

Previously, I was prepaid only, as I didn't see the use of spending 60-90 for phone service. I needed internet, and options were limited in my area. They offered me $40 for my phone and $40 for internet. This was a lie. My bill is $120, not $80, with all of the additional fees. On top of this, I brought a perfectly unlocked phone to them, and they had the audacity to lock my phone and refuse to unlock it when I wanted to go elsewhere. Had I known they would do this, I would of never gone with them to begin with. I will be going right back to my prepaid service and use someone else for internet. These companies think they can do whatever they want to you. What right do you have to lock a phone that isn't your's? It was fully paid off. We as consumers need to stand up or continue to be controlled by these big corporations. We let them do anything to us..
They should take back their own equipment from the store it was gotten from.

Representative at T'mobil made a bad mistake on 01/10/25 it was caught immediately. I called to speak to a manager,both were rude, and not helpful, I been a customer for 20 years thankfully no problems until now. The Rep was wrong, made an error, the managers admitted that but was still so rude and unprofessional. 
I am seeking another phone carrier.

T mobile is poor service.  The reps are not very knowledgeable.  Didn't even transfer all my phone contacts.  Very disappointed.  Need to change.  Wrote to home office.  No response.  They don't care.

I would have to agree with the reviews that accuse T-mobile of being scammers. I found out My 83 year old MIL was being charged for T-Mobile $130/month for half a year for a phone she had cancelled. I tried multiple times to stop the withdrawals from her bank account but they refused to stop billing her because she “couldn’t remember her passcode.” The bank claimed they cannot cancel those monthly debits direct from account. They said we’d need to contact the debitor and disturbingly said that T-mobile wasn’t good about complying, I.e. they are scammers. T mobile eventually said she could cancel by going into the store. By the time I saw my MIL over holidays and took her to store to cancel, she was prob out $600 which they wouldn’t refund. What a bull$&@t company.

I paid off my phone and I switched my service to a new company. I asked T Moblie to unlock my phone multiple times but it is locked for the second week. First time I called I spent 40 minutes on the phone with the T Mobile they told me that the phone is not purchased via T-Mobile so they would not unlock it. I explained that T-Mobile sold me the phone and I used to pay monthly to T Mobile for this phone. She replied that since the phone is not from T-Moble she cannot help me to unlock it. I had to explain again that I have all the paperwork in front of me showing that it was purchased from T-Mobile and in each bill it is stated that I paid for the phone with a final balance to be paid. She asked me to do some resetting and try to unlock it but with no result. Finally  I was informed that I would get an email, and the matter would be taken care of by the technical department and the phone would be unlocked within 72 hours. I did not get any email and after 4 days the phone was not unlocked. 
Next time I called I spent 80 minutes on the line with T Mobile going through settings on my phone and mostly waiting on line. The lady told me that the phone was not used so it cannot be unlocked. I explained that I have been using the phone for a year. She replied: “since the phone was not used the phone cannot be unlocked”  I had to repeat that I just said that I was using the phone for a year. And so on, and so on. Finally she said that the phone will be unlocked for sure this time in 24 hours. But nothing happened. We talked 2 days later and went thru the settings and I was told that the phone will be unlocked in 24 hours. It wasn't. I called 2 days later and I spent 2 hours on the phone with one lady and next another gentleman. Finally he asked me to go to the T-Mobile store. I explained that I already did go to the store but they said they don’t handle unlocking the phones and I have to call T-Mobile. But he explained that I need to go to another location so I did go to find the same reply, “we don’t handle the unlocking of phones, please call T-Mobile.

Negative 5 star review.   Was sold insurance and told at time of purchase of free phone replacement 4years later 20 amonth and a surprise 300 dollar for the free replacement phone.   Or I can upgrade from 300 for a worse model or 700.  Terrible scam

Review of T-Mobile Home Internet 15-Day Test Drive
Service Start: I began the 15-day trial with T-Mobile Home Internet in November. Initially, the service was poor, but after some adjustments, it significantly improved. I was impressed with the speed and quality of the service, to the point where I considered switching my lines from Xfinity to T-Mobile. However, due to the complexity of the process, I decided to cancel.

Cancellation Process: I spoke with customer service, and they recommended canceling within the 15-day period, which I did. I received a return label for the equipment and sent it back before the deadline.

Subsequent Issues: On November 29, I received a letter from the collections department for $55 with no explanation. I never received a bill from T-Mobile for this amount. Then, on December 9, 2024, I received another letter from a collection agency in PA for the same $55. Finally, on December 14, I received a bill for $32.66 for internet usage, despite having returned the equipment on time.

Reflection: I am referring this case to the consumer protection department in the state of Oregon for review. It is concerning that a large company like T-Mobile uses tactics like these without following proper procedures. I hope that with advances in artificial intelligence, new companies with better services and more transparent practices will emerge, eliminating the need to deal with situations like this.

only put 1 star cause you have to!!  I went in to their store in Waterville to do a free up grade, granted it was time to pay my regular bill, so i expected to walk out 55$ less! We decide on the phone do all the work the whole time telling me nothing about fee's, last I knew FREE was FREE, suddenly he writes down $84 circles it and says this is what we'll pay today with tax n service, i said thats kind of high isnt it, oh wait is that including my $55 current owed, he jumped on it, and reassured me yes yes thats it! look at my bank account today and to my surprise 2 charges $55 and $84. Went back in to speak to someone they were no help jus basically told me it was fee's and taxes plus a walk in service charge, even though the phone rep told me I had to go do it at a store. So no resolve I'm no happy and will be shopping for a better place!! been dealing with them 2/3 years, no late, no complaint!!
Got scammed. Internet did not work. Customer service is extremely lame. Store employees do not have authority
Terrible company owes me $1100, was rude; just terrible. Now dragging me along. Stay wat

edits.text: horrible company. piss poor coverage and rude personnel. when i paid them a year in advance they accused me of fraud. they are now dragging out mf refund. horrible company

text: 611 customer care has a glitch in it where it wont complete transaction. Then it will put you on hold for an hour!!!

Hidden Fees for watch reactivations. And when you cancel they don't tell you they'll send you a final bill that doesn't come till a month later and they tack on more fees. I was forced to pay my bill late. Terrible service and hard to cancel. 

11558051198
Horrible company, horrible service, worst customer service ever!

I ordered T-Mobile's home internet on October 17, 2024. Big mistake! No matter where I placed the gateway in our home, the gateway only showed one bar. What a joke! It wouldn't  provide a strong enough signal for even one of our smart TVs. Constant picture and sound lag. So I reinstated Spectrum internet. On Dec 26, I called and cancelled T-mobile. I was told the cancelation would not take effect until January 16, 2025. I argued that cancelation should take immediate effect, but to no avail. What a scam! This is their way of getting another full month of paid service despite the fact that I won't be using it from Dec 26th to when the cancelation takes effect on January 16th. This is no way for a company to earn consumer's trust and respect. I 
predict that T-Mobile's sketchy business practices will soon lead to its demise. A company just can't intentionally screw consumer's over and expect to remain in business.

10,000 ZEROS! I have never, ever been more angry than I am right now. T-Mobile bills change without warning. They changed my services and added extras. I had to call them at least 10 times before I could find anyone that could or would even attempt to understand me. 

Hours spent talking to human robots that do not do anything to resolve problems by design. The lack of service is unecessarily abusive. 

They should greet you with: "Welcome to T-Mobiles foreign-based customer torture chamber; how can we make your day more miserable? Do you enjoy repeating the same words over-and-over-and-over to barely English speaking people? Do you like being stuck in clown world vaccuum where nobody could hear you screaming? If so; T-Mobile is the place to be. 

That said; globalism reeks and so does T-Mobile.

We have had nothing but problems- dropping connectivity- had extra mesh installed, can't use or upload photos for work. The customer support is foreign and appears to read from a script/ is not a help. Multiple calls to customer support where they have an attitude. They ask the same questions over and over without logging the information or history. Cannot play video games etc- we lose internet continuously. After about a month , a different customer support said they would have continuous connectivity with our tower ( shouldn't it be already?) They say the same thing about the towers...And still the same issues again and again. Every time we call they ask the same questions wasting 20 or 30 minutes at a time like, our address, phone numbers etc...we have gotten hung up on or dropped from calls just to do it again. Once we have the opportunity we are changing our service to another company. 
After our call just today 3 January 2025 the lady finally admitted we were too far from our tower- so everyone else before knew that and kept taking our money. We only got one 1/2 of a month refund after complaining our faces off and now we finally know what the real issue is after 3 months! Apparently we are supposed to "call back if we have continuous problems from here further" Ridiculous and dishonest for them to obviously k own we wouldn't have connectivity where we are yet they allowed us to sign up. Total waste of time.
Worst customer service EVER !!!!  Go to any other phone provider, run go !!!

I have been a loyal customer with T-Mobile for over eight years always paid on time never had an issue with their billing. I tried to pay off a phone. The customer service representative told me the amount that I needed to close the account which never happened the phone remained locked, which I had already paid for And they charged me for two more months of service which they could see that I hadn’t used one minute of that service I called three separate times to be lied to to say it was taken care of and then I get a collection letter. I have a credit rating That is more important to me than paying them off.. And there’s no way I’m gonna let a hack job like T-Mobile screw it up so I paid them off again, but believe me I’m leaving bad reviews on every site that I see.
It’s not worth your time your money and your sanity to join this cell phone company. Their service sucks and I’m talking about cell service too.

NEVER AGAIN WILL I BE A CUSTOMER OF TMOBILE

Horrible experience. I have been a customer of TMobile for over 7 years with 4 open accounts and paid my bills on time. I have always had insurance to protect my phones and account. My phone broke on 12/24/24 and I went to the store. They said they could not do anything and go to the insurance. We called the insurance and they sent a new phone. I was on a call to transfer the information from the old phone to the new one for more than 3 hours and TMobile could not do it. They blamed the new phone and ordered a new one to be shipped to the store. They said it would take them about 15 minutes to transfer the information. I was just at the store and TMoblie put the wrong information on the phones and I could not get it transferred. I spent another 3 hours on the phone trying to figure out at the store and the only thing can do is order me another phone that will take another 4 days to arrive. I will be without a phone for more than a week and all TMobile wants to do is to give me a free cheap phone or to upgrade and pay more for a new phone.  The supervisor on the phone did nothing but blame the store or the first customer service representative and did not provide a solution. After 7 years I will bring my business elsewhere since TMobile does not care. It is all about the money to them!
Tmobile sucks and it keeps disconnecting don’t use tmobile if you want to have good connection

so here it is tmobile is over charging customers, here it is I have paid 1780 in fees and for services for the last 3 months to owe tmobile 1726 for services and fees to now being threaten to turn off my services for 200 dollars on a over payment they got in November and made adjustment on my billed to still charge it and get a message stating I have a past due for December 16 of 212 dollars to pay 300 on that 212.00 to owe them now 600 dollars! That’s a rip off! The supervisor said it well the 212 was past due but it was incorrect! How? How can a company say you owe them 212 in past due fees on the 16 of December to get a payment of 300 on the 23rd of December to now owe you 600 on the 25  and they turning my services off! Sad Tmobile, so the supervisor stated to now having to pay us more to get your phone back on to over charge again! this is not right!!

Have had T-Mobile internet for about 2 years. We live in a rural area and have tried several other options. Have had great service out here at 5G levels. 
Occasionally, the speed drops and I either have to reset my router or call them. There is usually something they can do on their end to increase my speed and they gave me a tip the last time I called for how to split my network that helped. 
Had to replace my router last year when nothing we tried resolved the issue, but they did all that with no problem. 
Overall, I'm pretty happy with them.

Tried to cancel internet before they sent me the modem. Told me I had to wait 24 hours but it would be cancelled and "rest assured It would be cancelled and I wouldn't be charged". Ended up on hold for 30mins the next day trying to make sure it's cancelled before I gave up. They sent it anyway. Now I need to figure out how to return this thing without being charged. It's ridiculous.

This is the worst customer service I’ve ever seen.  They sold me internet that never worked and never told me any time during a full month of tech calls and hours of my time that what they sold me would never work.  No internet for a month and gipted me out of a $200 rebate.  I warn anyone considering using T-Mobile internet to steer away.

I would give "0" if I could.  I am not complaining about the service across the country, although much improvement is necessary even on Interstates Freeways.  Instead, I am complaining about false advertisements for deals and rates.  As a senior citizen I chose to go with T-Mobile for its over 55 rates and I also chose to upgrade our phones because we were long overdue for new phones.  We went through Costco because not only were they giving $400 dollars per trade-in for each of our phones, but a Costco gift card on top of that (which they did). One year and seven months later, we are still paying for the phones, and it turns out to be FULL PRICE without the $400 trade-in. Their Customer Service is useless because I called about the bill within the first six months, and they said that our bills would reflect the $800 for the two trade-ins in due time.  Well, the bills continue at full rate. How dare they take advantage of seniors.

Just awful shop staff are incompetent changed my plan without telling me . Customer service the worst I have ever experienced took 9 days and close to 10hrs to restore my plan  plus charged me more than double the original plan cost ! 
Phone service was great store  staff the worst and customer service the worst

In December, I upgraded my phone but decided to return it under their policy and purchased a different one. My trade-in had already been processed, but after extensive conversations in-store and online, I was assured by multiple T-Mobile representatives that the bill credits from the first purchase would transfer to the second.

Fast forward a year, and I’ve discovered that wasn’t true. I didn’t notice the missing credits sooner because my bill is already higher than other providers. When I reached out, T-Mobile apologized (Chat Reference ID: 2cd9bf0e-5733-44bc-aae8-ceeffd5fa014) and offered to start issuing the credits now—spread over the next two years until December 2026. But this doesn’t address the almost $500 already stolen from me because multiple people lied to me about transferring the trade in device credits.

Under the original agreement, my phone would have been paid off by December 2025, and my bill would have been $40–50 cheaper per month. Instead, I’m stuck paying for their mistake.

T-Mobile, I want a refund for the money you owe me, not just future credits. This is unacceptable.

Their customer service is awful. I cancel their overpriced cell service to get a better deal with a different provider. They still took money from my account. I called and explained to them that I cancelled the service, but they insisted they were right. They were not right. They keep sending me notices that I have a credit that will be applied to my next bill. How can that be when I don't have an account. I have called multiple times to get a refund and I'm given the run around and never get the money I am owed. Today I had to jump through more hoops to finally get the promise of a prepaid card mailed to me. The money NEVER should have left my bank account! I am glad to not be a customer with this company that thinks it is okay to steal from a person's bank account and then have the audacity to make them prove that the money was wrongfully taken out of their account. If you had the correct system of accounts, you would be able to see on a closed account that the customer is owed a refund. Start taking accountability and show your customers some respect. This ordeal has been many months in the making. Glad it is over. I do not recommend this company at all!

edits.text: I closed my account and they still took $70 from my bank account. The whole reason I dropped this company is because they keep raising their prices. I used to get $10 a month in discounts and they raised the price $10 so I don't actually get a discount anymore. I can't afford this much per month. I went with someone cheaper with the same coverage and service. When I tried to resolve this, they said they couldn't do anything.

text:
T-Mobile cheats me when I went to the store on Black Friday at 8:00AM. The saleman told me about T-Mobile Internet and asked me to try out for two weeks free.  I agreed and took the brand new modem home.  By 6:45PM, I returned to the store and returned the new modem back without opening the box.  I told him I'm not interested.  So, I did a paper work and printed me a receipt.  

Now T-mobile charged me $218.20 including $160 monthly phone service. Don't ever try out with their offer.

T-Mobile advertised I can get discount for my old Iphone trade-in for $150. After all transactions, I received an email I only got $45 and I need to get another $90 from Apple.  I canceled my order.

Don't buy Iphone from T-Mobile or Phone Service provider. Buy directly from Apple store.  The store does not have what you need and they need to order anyway.

"I recently switched my phone service from Pure Talk to T-Mobile and had the pleasure of being assisted by Scott Wallace at the Waterville, Maine location. I was incredibly impressed with his professionalism and helpfulness. Scott was extremely knowledgeable about the different plans and patiently answered all of my questions. He made the entire process of switching carriers very easy and stress-free. I highly recommend Scott and the T-Mobile store in Waterville."

Just found out that they changed their policy, when the account is suspended they still charge the full amount.  Last year it was a not so reasonable $10/month, now it is just a ripoff!   Will return the gateway modem from now on, and need it will go pick up a new one....

I was a customer for 3yrs with Crooked T-Mobile. Giving up my account for their convenience did me no good. Still”they took their payment April 4th. By the 5th my service was terminated. It was hell from that date on. The whole month they’re sending Tickets to locate the payment. By the end of the month I was drained. Moving on to another carrier was my only option. But”not with slimy T-mobile one can’t move on. They blocked my iPhone as if I owed them money I had prepaid pay as I go. And”it is now 12/23/24 those Crooks never reimburse me my $67.08. I’m on fixed income I’m not rich”I went out a bought Tshirts that reads. I was Robbed by T-Mobile. Join the movement these big companies are to powerful with our money.

After 20 plus years with this company they did nothing to make things easier. After my rate kept increasing there were no deals to keep my bill at a decent rate for just one line. I switched over to Xfinity and tried to use my Google pixel (that had been paid off completely) and keep my number. I have been waiting over a week to get them to unlock my phone so I can use it with Xfinity. Ever time I call it's the same thing, "they're waiting for business solutions". I have been told it takes 10 minutes at the most to unlock a phone. And I have been waiting this long. T-Mobile does not care about their customers. They do nothing to help you out. I really don't agree that they would be voted best customer service.

I'm a long time customer (12+ years) so I guess I'm satisfied with their service.  I'm in a plan that they no longer offer, but am able to keep as long as I stay with them, so no price increases, except "fees", which is five unlimited lines for under $150/month.  The service itself is generally good as no one on the plan lives in a remote area.  Customer service on their "800" number has also been decent overall.  They have moved to offshore agents, but my experience has been decent to very good with them.  Going into their stores is a mixed bag, but never any horrible experiences, though some better than others.  
Another major carrier has recently offered me new phones + gift card$ to switch, but I'm reluctant to take the bait, at least for now sticking with the "devil I know" as opposed to the one I don't know.
Clair is very patient and helpful! She’s indeed five star

It's unbelievable how difficult it is to get it is to get anything done with their customer service. I signed up for 2 business internet lines, 2 business phone lines with trade-ins. I have spent collectively 7 hours on the phone with sales and various customer service departments just trying to get the lines set up, phones traded in, and business internet set up.

I'm 70 years old and never have I had a company promise (voice and written) to refund money for a mistake they made, and then take it back!  When I first approached this with T-mobile, they wanted to add the overcharged amount to my account.  However, I wanted them to credit the amount to my credit card instead. After one month discussing this with four different representatives (and hours wasting my time waiting on the phone), I spoke to a manager.  She apologized for the inconvenience and trouble I had experienced with this issue.  She said she would not only refund the money to my credit card but would add another $16 and, in addition, I would not have to pay the next month's bill for $16. She also said she would send a confirmation email, and follow-up with me in one week with a phone call. Sounds good, right?  Well, she did email me the refund confirmation and they did cancel the next month's bill.  However, she never followed up with the phone call, and after a month, the refund still had not appeared on my credit card.  When I called to inquire about this, I was told the company decided to revoke the refund.  A multibillion dollar company revokes a $27.24 promised refund.  How's that for great customer service?!  I'll be quitting T-mobile and would not recommend them to anyone thinking of obtaining their service.  Their word means nothing.  Greedy with no ethical guidelines.

The people at my local store, refused to help me carry two small boxes inside, from my car. I told them I was physically disabled and all three said no. I had to struggle getting the boxes in. I also called earlier, and the girl gave me attitude like she was bothered by me calling asking for help. The females that work in Walker County, Al. are horrible. They all have attitudes.

I had a great experience at the Cherry Hill NJ Experience Center.  I would have given it 5 stars, but the phone support was awful before going to the store.
Suggest for other T-Mobile users with serious issues that you find a local store considered a T-Mobile experience center.

Kevin, the store manager, is fantastic!  His staff was also well qualified and excellent to work with.

My phone number was hijacked.  T-Mobile phone support was awful and non-responsive.  After wasting 3 days and countless hours of phone calls, I met Kevin.  He resolved the issue, and his team got me on my way quickly.

This company's phone service is terrible. There customer service is shit. They lie to get you in. I canceled my service in August and I am here with another bill in November on hold. These people should be shut down. They are a bunch of theives

Thank you so much to all of you who took the time to share your negative experiences with us I appreciate it.  Because of the reviews and not one response from TMobile I have decided to go with another carrier.  So many reasons why I couldn't trust them ever I would say.  Happy Holidays.  Best.

edits.text: If I could give a zero that'd be more appropriate for this review.  I'm on a family plan and was interested in staying with TMobile but wanted off the plan, stay with TMobile and get a new number.  I researched their plans and decided to go with the 55 plus plan as I will be 66 this Friday.  The person at the store was not very interested in helping me or offering any feedback.  Frustrated I  said, "ok I'll go with AT&T".  Want to know what he said, "ok, good luck"  The entire time I was with him he seemed more interested in what he was doing on the computer than helping me so I left.  Went to another carrier and notified my friend who said that's disappointing the lack of interest I was given and she will process this and may follow me to the other carrier.  She's been with TMobile for years but was very disappointed in the lack of professional customer Service and may leave herself.  That's all.

text: Very likely the worst customer service amongst telecom companies. Lost my phone while traveling in Europe, and tried to get a new SIM card to install in a temporary phone one of my family members gave me. Spent €180 in phone calls with them, in which I was told that the first SIM i was sent was not the correct one because it had a protection feature that could not be removed; and after the supposedly “correct” second SIM was received, ran into exactly the same problem. The reps on the phone don’t seem to know anything that has to do with international travel situations, and they refuse to let you talk to supervisors and managers. It’s a total disaster…

Your Techs didn't want to change my email address on my account to remedy my issue . No explanation just that it will redo everything.  What is everything? They did take the time to call me and repeat six times that they don't control Apple and that Apple hasn't hinge to do with them, providing  me an add-on that isn't working. The tmobile tech also called me at 10 pm to repeat the same thing, again! I was sleeping at that time! Now I am angry! Your company sucks with lazy minds, and I will tell the world why I feel this way!

Won't go into big story, this company is a money making scamming franchise. I know of a few law suits on them now. The bad thing is the fcc, the US government and others choose to ignore and let them do what they do. This is why we have bad things going on in this world today. Yes some folks are allowed free reign ,however Don't let a no one like me try to some illegal crap, I'll sit in jail until I rot. TMobile is a bunch of crooks and scammers. But their day is coming!!!!😁

I used to be a fervent T-Mobile supporter. I must have recommended them 100 times, but that company no longer exists. The change was gradual, but lately, it feels like every decision has been anti-consumer. 

My biggest problem is the disconnect between the store and corporate levels. The stores are like the Wild West. You may find an upstanding person to help you, but more likely, you will encounter an outlaw who will, at best, misrepresent and, at worst, outright lie to get you to do the thing I assume will get them paid.

If the call center had been helpful, that would have been different, but alas, those days are gone, too. If you need to call them because someone lied to you, I will save you some time. They will say, “I’m so sorry you feel misled, but you should have read the contract, so it’s on you.” 

While I agree it is on the consumer to read the contract, it feels very disingenuous to call yourself the “un-carrier” or say, “We changed wireless for our customers” while treating people just as bad if not worse than the other guys. I have hit my limit and will leave after 12 years of patronage. No one cared there, so I guess I won’t either.

Last fall, my husband added a line to my phone, so total was 2 lines.Then T-mobile kept charging me 3 line for 12 monthes and I haD to call monthly for correct bill even they said bill fixed. So I cancelled one line at November 2024 before the bill due day. then they charge me $160.01 for one line. before was $50 per line. last month I got the refund, this month they charge me again the same extra bill. I have to call the bank to dispute. don't waste your time on this SCAM company !!!

Worst phone carrier ever, do not waste your time. I switched from Verizon for 8 days, had the most horrible phone service I’ve ever experienced, called in to switch back and was told my four lines were closed out and no balance on the account. A month later I receive a phone call from T-Mobile saying I owe $246, as I’ve called in and spoke to someone and their manager they refuse to make it right and because it wasn’t closed out properly (somehow that’s my fault) they are leaving a balance on the account until I pay it. I will never be using T-Mobile for the rest of my life and I don’t recommend it for anyone.
Wifi goes out every 15 minutes. They’ve claimed to be “fixing” it multiple times and I’ve seen little to 0 progress. Do better.

Companies a THIEF helping themselves with other people's bank accounts after REFUSING to issue a confirmation number.. after I've been a customer for 15 years..spend your money elsewhere.. would NEVER recommend..
Deceptive.  Offer rebates and promotions that they do not honor.  There’s a class action lawsuit dated 12/8/24 for this.  I hope I can join.  There’s a reason they have 1.5 stars across the board.

I bought an iPhone for the first time. It was carrier locked through T-Mobile so I called the customer service and the snarky lady on the other end kept asking for an account number though I explained twice that I didn’t have service with them and didn’t understand why they could not use the iMei like every other company I had gone through to look into the issue. The woman was rude and didn’t provide any other info as to what I could do.
Needless to say I have a phone I can’t use and I wouldn’t use T-Mobile if they were the last company on earth, I’d write letters and mail them first. Thanks for nothing T-Mobile.

I have been a customer of T-Mobile‘s for nine years. I went into the store today after being told I had a $3000 credit limit to upgrade my iPhone 12 to iPhone 16 Pro Max and was told essentially because although I make my payment on time every month I split the payments up into two payments. I’m being judged and now they want me to pay a total of $900 for two phones so I will be leaving T-Mobile.

edits.text: I have been with T-Mobile seven years and I always have a payment arrangement every month because I’m a senior citizen on a fixed income .I was not able to keep the payment arrangement and only needed it to be extended by one day, which is  24 hours and they weren’t willing to accommodate me at all after seven years this is the first time they’ve ever denied me an extension!! I guess because they’ve gotten the monopoly on cell phone companies  now and all they are concerned with is making money. Thank God I don’t have a contract with them and I will never get another contract with them and as far as purchasing iPhone 15’s I will definitely be purchasing them through another company . This company offers No empathy at all,  no care or concern, and the managers are the worst.

text: Stopped in a T Mobile store last week to inquire about current promotions that they had. I walked out with a deal that I couldn't say no to and they put it in writing. However a few hours later after talking with T Mobile Customer Service I found out that they performed a Bait and Switch tactic and was signed up for a plan all totally different and much more expensive then was agreed to and put in writing. They told me I was welcome to return the equipment back to the store. When I attempted to return it the next day, T Mobile told me that it is their policy to charge a restocking fee which was huge, over $100.00. I refused told them that I was going to keep their equipment until I receive a call from a higher up supervisor in the company but as of the date so far I never received a phone call after repeated attempts and I am still holding on to their equipment. Needless to say I will never do business with T Mobile ever again

Kelly Handyman Services LLC led by Brandon & Fabian are true professionals.
Their work is top shelf and far above the norm. Do not hesitate do call them as they will deliver superior customer service.
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ ⭐️

edits.text:
Today I visited the T-Mobile store across from Town Center in Jacksonville, Florida.

I was greeted with a smile and incredible service that was offered by Brenda. Brenda is new at this location however she has been with T-Mobile for years.

Congratulations!

text: Great service..........butttttttttttt. ... Lots of hidden fees, I'm paying more for disconnect fees then my actual bill. I can't keep up . They turn my services off 3/4 days before I get paid every month that's 20$ per line restoration charge (To click a button)? And also why do we have to pay 10$ for a live rep to take a payment? Aren't they working for T-Mobile? Or the customers? I literally pay them 150/200 every 2 weeks and still 4/500 behind because they are constantly suspending my service. On top of them always "upgrading in my area" and my lines are down in that time frame. Have to constantly get in touch with tech Support to deduct 20/30 dollars off my bill ohhhh that's soooo much huh! T mobile is a rip off to say the least!

Last year, I was influenced by a T-Mobile ad. I went to their website and it says my rural area in middle Ga, 30 miles outside Macon Ga, was a strong signal area. I ordered their SIM card, and it gave me zero bars. Thankfully, I was refunded. I now have 1 bar (always) with an ATT SIM card.

edits.text:
Horrible. 3 yrs ago, tried their service, and again in 2022, because they said service improved. Wrong!

Each time, rural Georgia showed complete coverage, but we couldn't make a single phone call and mostly had 0 bars. When we switched back to AT&T, we had 1 to 2 bars (PS, Verizon also works, but AT&T works in more areas here).
Anyway, not for us.

text: STAY AWAY. A SCAM. In the USA:  I switched to them, found out within two days that I couldn't get coverage at my home or office so closed account.  For SIX months, I've been trying to get a refund for the money I paid since the coverage didn't work. NONE.  Been working on this, they never call you back.  Today, just found out despite escalation there is a clause that says even if you don't use the service you owe that amount.  I switched because I have several friends that have used them for decades all over the world without any problems. DON'T BE TEMPTED.  STAY AWAY.  THEY WILL NEVER HELP YOU.

No one cares or knows what they are doing. I have a mechanical issue with my phone and have been bounced around between T-Mobile and the insurance company, Assurant, a million times. Then, they finally agree that it is on T-Mobile to replace it. I filed my replacement over the phone and it said I would get a replaced in 24 hours. 48 hours later, no phone, so I call, get bounced around, and find out it was denied, but no one told me. Then, I have to trouble shoot including factory resetting my phone, which is fine. It doesn't work and I am told a replacement will be sent and it will be an upgrade as mine is not available. I don't get an email in 24 hours as they say I will, so I call 30 hours later to find out a replacement order was never put in. This time it is replaced, but they won't honor the upgrade. They say they will review the phone logs and get back to me regarding what the rep promised me. A few days later, no call, so I contact them again. I promised they will review the phone logs and get back to me. Three days later, nothing, so I contact them again. Supposedly, she called, but she didn't leave a message or make in any notes in my account. No one is willing to look at the calls, contact either rep that said they would look at the calls, or contact the rep who made the promise. About 10 days dealing with this. After almost 20 years with T-Mobile and I am walking away and will NEVER EVER come back.

I became a New T-Mobile customer in December of 2023. I got reeled into getting the 5G Home Cell internet service on a trial basis. I got COVID and was unable to bring the unite back in until after the trial period ended, on January 23, 2024. I brought it back because it sucked worse than my DSL. I live in a rural area. T-Mobile did not give a crap & said I would be charged a prorate for the time I had it. Fine, I just didn't want the service and so I asked the T-Mobile rep to cancel it. I also called T-Mobile right after, to let them know to cancel it. T-Mobile's website was problematic. Many times, I was kicked off the website or the website was non-responsive and I was unable to see my account online and so I gave up. I trusted that T-Mobile would charge me for just the cell phone service I signed up for. BIG MISTAKE! I was able to get online without a problem 2 days ago and noticed that T-Mobile is still charging me for equipment I don't have and service I cancelled, that I cannot even utilize without the equipment I turned into T-Mobile back on January 23, 2024. I immediately called T-Mobile and their customer service rep said that they have record of me returning the equipment on January 23, 2024 but they don't have any record of the separate call to them I made, cancelling the 5 G internet service that I cannot even use without the equipment I turned into them. T-Mobile is refusing to refund the $540.00 of false charges over the past 12 months. the T-Mobile rep blamed me for the charges for the service, I was not receiving, because I did not monitor my account & that I did not call T-Mobile to let them know I was being charged for a service I cancelled on January 23, 2024. I am on auto-payment and paperless billing that the T-Mobile's customer service representative sold me on and gave me discounts for agreeing to use, when I signed up. I feel that T-Mobile promotes auto pay & paperless billing on purpose, so that you don't catch T-Mobile right away, in their theft. These charges are fraudulent and T-Mobile knowingly stole my money for equipment and service T-Mobile knows I didn't have and am not even able to use without their equipment. My 5G T-Mobile cell internet # was 928-266-7382. I have filed a complaint with the FCC & the FTC. I will look online to see what other agencies I can file a complaint with, to get my stolen money back.

I was quoted a price of $150/month for a new cellphone plan (including two new phones) in-store (3315 Westpark Dr, Houston, TX 77005) and the price I’m actually paying is $90/month higher than what they quoted me. I’ve called customer service and the store multiple times and no one has solved my issue. This is outrageous. It was deceptive. It was a total bait-n-switch. They need to be held accountable for what they promise.
Worse customer service and always steals money from customers through overcharged bills

If you want the too long don't want to read.  Do not use T Mobile.  They are a terrible company.  Pay the extra 10-15 a month per line for a real telecom provider.

T Mobile as a company is simply not in compliane with Federal Trade Communication guidelines and generally does not adhear to business practices set by the local Chambers of Commerce for each and every region within the United States they operate.

I decided to try out the service, it bare functioned.  The modem died, I tried to get it repaired.  They added to much friction to that process and actively refused to repair the T Mobile 5G modem.  Come today.  I have a $500 USD bill for services not rendered as they were keeping the account running for what appears to be 6 months even though it's a month to month contract (auto renew without consent is against several FTC guidelines and policies).  They made it very difficult if not impossible to cancel my account.  They've designed so that you have to call them and they spend 30 minutes trying to keep you as a customer.  I'm sure I cancelled the account in the past but here it is.

I called today to see if they could make it right.  They made course of action except to pay the debt collection agency.  Their service was sub par and quite frankly it was a terrible experience overall.  If you operate an Authorized T-Mobile reseller or repair center.  Quite frankly I would find a more honest company.  The negative karma you're generating working for T-Mobile will come back to haunt you.

A final note.  To any open source projects such as OpenStack or any other project managed by the CNCF or OpenInfra Foundation.  Please do not accept code commits from a company which does not operate ethically and within the guidelines of what I believe are pretty normal business practices.  Their customer service personal are trained to "handle" customers, they are not trained to resolve issues.

Switched to a much cheaper service with far superior coverage but can't stop payment at t-mobile because I can't remember the PIN I set over 2 years ago.  So just hop on the t-mobile app and reset my PIN as per their call center IVR ‐ sounds easy right?  Nope, since I'm no longer a customer, I cannot reset my PIN!  Go to the store to find out there are things on my account that don't apply to us like home internet and an iPad, we never had either.  Have we been paying for those things?  Your guess is as good as mine and theirs, they really don't know.  Without a PIN, absolutely no one can help me.  We just paid what we paid and we're still paying and there's quite literally nothing that can be done about it.  No one in the local store and no one in customer service can help me despite all of them being able to tell we canceled the service so I have to pay $40 to stop pay at the bank level.  If you're in the market, find any other company on the planet but this one.

I’ve been experiencing nonstop issues with T-Mobile. I no longer have service with them, yet they continue to withdraw money from my account every month. When I request them to remove my credit card information from the account, they claim that I no longer have an account, so they cannot access it to remove the information. As a result, they just keep taking money from my account. This situation is absurd and unreasonable, and when I do manage to speak with a customer service representative, the issues persist.

F-n HATE this company. I had Sprint since Sprint was Nextel. Then T-Mobile bought Sprint and promised that Sprint customers would go to TMobile towers - LIE #1. They used the same old nonworking Sprint towers. I couldn't even get cell service inside or outside my own condo. And that's a problem as a First Responder in FL, esp during the pandemic and hurricane season when I'm on call. Thankfully I had a separate work phone (Verizon) that worked. As for my responder discount, I've been trying since Nov 2024 (it is now Dec 2024) to verify it using the whackass busted app called T-Life and I'm even on the phone trying to get a person for an hour. Also, upgrades were supposed to come with Costco bucks, but I ain't seen mine yet. Two tin cups with a string would work better than this. Everything about this company is broke, busted and utter crap. GO ELSEWHERE.

Terrible customer service I upgraded router to the new one and sent the old one back with ups  now they saying that the was damage because the little paper sticker in the back is scratched and they charged me  79.00 dollars for it . Impossible to resolve issue. Supervisors are useless  and uncaring . I'm ready to drop the whole service . Feels like scam

In July, I signed up for internet service but then learned their speed was inadequate for my needs. I cancelled within 48 hours but was told that the contract gave them 60 days to make the cancellation effective during which time they would bill my card. Customer service assured me that after those 2 months all payments would be refunded to my card. It is now December- 5 months later - and no refund. I called and was told that they will now process the refund. I asked why it had taken so long and was told that they don’t ever make the refund until the customer calls and asks for it. These people are snakes. I will never do business with them again.

T mobile service is 💯 Horrible in my location ,, they promised last 4 years no worry we will have your service back working ,, unstable connection means never completely connecting to internet thus sucking data non stop.... they changed my contract from 100 gb to 50 but tells me it was always 50gb....what a big lie...
